businessSummary.oneLiner: You have a strong 4.4-star reputation with 297 Google reviews in El Paso. But your website may be costing you HVAC calls. We found several issues that could be sending potential customers to competitors instead.

The phone number on your website doesn't match your Google Business Profile, which can push you lower in local search results
Google may be treating your website as two separate sites, splitting your ranking power in half
businessSummary.competitivePosition: You have a strong reputation — 297 reviews at 4.4 stars in El Paso is hard to beat. But your website isn't reflecting that. Competitors with cleaner sites and visible reviews are capturing calls that should be going to you.
businessSummary.seasonalUrgency: AC demand in El Paso spikes by April — earlier than most markets. Every week these gaps stay open is another week competitors capture the emergency calls and replacement jobs that should be yours.
businessSummary.recommendedFirstProject: Align phone number across your website and Google Business Profile so customers and Google see the same business. Set up a single website address so Google stops splitting your ranking between two versions.
businessSummary.growthOpportunity: Full website rebuild with pages targeting each city you serve (El Paso, Las Cruces, Socorro, Horizon City), a review widget showing your Google reviews, online scheduling for after-hours bookings, and ongoing optimization to keep climbing in local search results.

gapSummary.gap: 297 Google reviews not showing on your website
gapSummary.impact: You've earned a 4.4-star rating with 297 reviews — that's a real competitive advantage in El Paso. But none of them appear on your website. When a homeowner is comparing two HVAC companies side by side, they pick the one showing real customer feedback. Right now, visitors have to leave your site and search Google separately to see what your customers think of you.

gapSummary.gap: Phone number on website doesn't match Google
gapSummary.impact: The phone number on your website is different from the one listed on your Google Business Profile. Google uses this information to verify and rank local businesses — when the numbers don't match, Google has less confidence in your listing. This can push you lower in local search results right when El Paso homeowners are searching for HVAC help.

gapSummary.gap: Website loads at two different addresses
gapSummary.impact: Your website works at both weneedair.com and www.weneedair.com — but there's nothing telling Google which one is the real one. Google may see these as two separate websites competing against each other. Instead of one strong listing, your ranking power is split between two copies. A simple redirect fixes this in minutes.

gapSummary.gap: No clear way to call or book above the fold
gapSummary.impact: When someone lands on your homepage, there's no obvious next step — no prominent 'Call Now', 'Book Online', or 'Get a Quote' button visible without scrolling. In El Paso's heat, a homeowner with a broken AC isn't going to scroll around looking for how to reach you. They'll hit the back button and call whoever makes it easiest.

gapSummary.gap: Very little content for Google to work with
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage has about 800 words of content. Competitors with more detailed pages covering services, service areas, credentials, and common questions tend to rank higher because Google sees them as more helpful and authoritative. More useful content on your site means more chances for El Paso customers to find you instead of a competitor.

gapSummary.gap: No emergency or after-hours messaging
gapSummary.impact: HVAC emergencies don't wait for business hours. There's no '24/7', 'Same-Day Service', or 'Emergency' messaging anywhere on your site. When a homeowner's AC dies at 10 PM, they call whoever looks like they're available — and right now, your website doesn't say that's you.

gapSummary.gap: No way to book online
gapSummary.impact: There's no way for customers to schedule an appointment from your website. Most people prefer to book when it's convenient for them — including late at night or on weekends when your phone may not be answered. After-hours visitors go to whichever competitor lets them book at 11 PM.

gapSummary.gap: No pages targeting cities you serve
gapSummary.impact: There are no pages specifically about the cities you serve. When someone searches 'AC repair El Paso' or 'HVAC Las Cruces', your site doesn't have a dedicated page to match that search. Competitors with location-specific pages show up in those results — you don't.

gapSummary.gap: Google can't read your business details from the code
gapSummary.impact: Your website's code doesn't clearly tell Google what type of business you are, where you're located, or what hours you're open. This structured information helps Google connect your website to your Business Profile and show you in local search results. Without it, Google has to guess — and guessing means lower confidence in your listing.

gapSummary.gap: Most images have no descriptions for Google
gapSummary.impact: 20 of 27 images on your site have no text descriptions. Google can't understand what these images show, so they can't help your rankings. The 3 images that do have descriptions use irrelevant stock photo text like 'black and white bed linen' — not exactly helpful for an HVAC company.

gapSummary.gap: No financing information for big-ticket replacements
gapSummary.impact: A full HVAC system replacement costs $5,000-$15,000 — one of the biggest purchases a homeowner makes. If your website doesn't mention financing, homeowners may assume they can't afford your services and call the competitor who advertises 0% APR instead.

gapSummary.gap: Website reloads from scratch every visit
gapSummary.impact: Your website forces every visitor's browser to re-download the entire page every time they visit. If a homeowner checks your site today and comes back tomorrow to call, they wait for the full load again. Competitor sites load instantly the second time around.

gapSummary.gap: Google search listing has a formatting error
gapSummary.impact: Your description in Google search results ends with a stray quotation mark — a small but visible error. When homeowners scan search results, this makes your listing look less polished than competitors with clean descriptions. First impressions in search results determine who gets the click.

gapSummary.gap: No blog or helpful content
gapSummary.impact: Without any articles on your site, you miss searches like 'How much does AC repair cost in El Paso?' or 'When should I replace my AC unit?' Competitors who answer these common questions are capturing customers you'll never see — because your site has nothing to show for those searches.

gapSummary.gap: No license number displayed
gapSummary.impact: Texas requires TDLR/TACL licensing for HVAC work. Not showing your license number makes your business look less established than competitors who display theirs prominently. It's a quick trust signal that homeowners check, especially for big jobs.

gapSummary.gap: Shared links show no preview image
gapSummary.impact: When someone shares your website link on Facebook, in a text message, or anywhere else — no preview image appears. It looks like a broken link. Competitors with branded preview images get more clicks from every share and referral.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: Your website tells Google you have 4 reviews instead of 82, four duplicate versions of your site are splitting your ranking power, and there's no way to book online
impactEstimate.installNote: If even 1 of those was a system replacement, that's another $8,000-$15,000.

businessSummary.oneLiner: You have an excellent 4.9-star reputation with 82 Google reviews in Orlando. But your website may be costing you HVAC calls. We found several issues that could be sending potential customers to competitors instead.
topThreeGaps: Your website tells Google you only have 4 reviews — but you actually have 82, which makes Google question the accuracy of your site
Google sees 4 separate copies of your website, splitting your ranking power into quarters instead of building one strong listing
You have zero tracking installed — no way to know how many people visit your site, where they come from, or if your marketing is working
businessSummary.competitivePosition: You have a near-perfect reputation — 82 reviews at 4.9 stars in Orlando is outstanding. But your website has gaps that are giving competitors an unfair advantage. Companies with worse reviews but better websites are showing up ahead of you.
businessSummary.seasonalUrgency: AC season in Orlando runs nearly year-round, but demand spikes hard in April through September. Every week these issues stay unfixed is another week competitors with cleaner sites capture the emergency calls and system replacements that should be yours.
businessSummary.recommendedFirstProject: Fix the review count mismatch so Google sees your real 82 reviews, set up redirects so all four versions of your site point to one address, and install Google Analytics so you can finally see what's happening on your website.
businessSummary.growthOpportunity: Add online booking for after-hours leads, a live Google Reviews widget showing your 4.9-star rating, a blog targeting Orlando HVAC questions, and a financing page to close more system replacement jobs.

gapSummary.gap: Your website tells Google you have 4 reviews — you actually have 82
gapSummary.impact: The hidden code Google reads claims you have just 4 reviews with a perfect 5.0 rating. But your actual Google profile shows 82 reviews at 4.9 stars. This mismatch makes Google suspicious of your site's accuracy. You've earned 82 real reviews — your website should be showing Google the truth, not a wrong number that hurts your credibility.

gapSummary.gap: 4 separate versions of your website are competing against each other
gapSummary.impact: Your site loads at four different addresses: with http, https, www, and non-www. All four show the same page with no redirect to a single version. Google sees this as four separate websites competing against each other. Instead of one strong listing, your ranking power is split four ways. A competitor with one clean address gets the full benefit — you get a quarter.

gapSummary.gap: No way to know if your website generates any calls
gapSummary.impact: Your website has zero tracking — no Google Analytics, no Search Console, nothing. You have no idea how many people visit, which pages they look at, or where they come from. Every dollar you spend on marketing could be wasted and you would never know. This is like running a business with no bookkeeper.

gapSummary.gap: No way to book online
gapSummary.impact: When a homeowner's AC breaks at 11 PM in Orlando, they want to book service right now — not wait until morning to call. You have no way for customers to schedule appointments online. Competitors who offer a 'Book Now' button are capturing those late-night leads while your phone goes to voicemail.

gapSummary.gap: Only 6 reviews shown on your website — you have 82 on Google
gapSummary.impact: You're sitting on 82 Google reviews at 4.9 stars — that's a massive trust advantage in Orlando. But your website only shows 6 generic-looking testimonials. A visitor who doesn't check Google first sees a business that looks like it just started, not one with near-perfect reviews.

gapSummary.gap: Your site is missing all security protections
gapSummary.impact: Your website is missing every standard security protection that modern sites have. This means your site could be loaded inside someone else's website, browsers don't enforce secure connections strictly, and Google may flag security concerns. Competitors with proper security settings get a small but real ranking boost.

gapSummary.gap: No blog or helpful articles
gapSummary.impact: You have zero articles on your site. In Orlando's competitive HVAC market, blog content is how you show up for questions like 'why is my AC blowing warm air Orlando' or 'best SEER rating for Florida homes.' Without a blog, you only rank for your company name and a few service pages. Competitors who answer common questions are capturing customers you'll never see.

gapSummary.gap: No financing information for system replacements
gapSummary.impact: AC replacement in Orlando costs $5,000-$15,000. If a homeowner can't afford it upfront, they call the competitor who advertises '$89/month financing available.' You mention nothing about financing anywhere on your site — that's a big-ticket job walking out the door.

gapSummary.gap: No way to track which marketing brings in calls
gapSummary.impact: Without call tracking, you can't tell which marketing channel drives phone calls. If you spend money on Google Ads, SEO, or Nextdoor, you have no way to measure what's working and what's wasting money. You're making marketing decisions blind.

gapSummary.gap: No Florida license number displayed
gapSummary.impact: Florida homeowners look for contractor license numbers before hiring — especially for big jobs. Not showing your license makes your business look less established than competitors who display theirs prominently in the footer of every page.

gapSummary.gap: Shared links show no preview image
gapSummary.impact: When someone shares your website on Facebook, Nextdoor, or in a text message, no preview image appears. It shows as a plain text link with no visual. Posts with images get more than double the engagement — every share without a preview is a missed opportunity.

gapSummary.gap: Images are unoptimized and slow to load
gapSummary.impact: Your images are in an older format that's about twice the file size they need to be. On a slow phone connection, these images delay how fast your page appears. Modern image formats would cut the file size in half with no quality loss.

gapSummary.gap: No pricing information anywhere on the site
gapSummary.impact: Homeowners searching 'AC repair cost Orlando' won't find your site. You mention no prices, no ranges, and no 'starting at' figures anywhere. People want to know what to expect before they call — and competitors who share pricing ranges are building trust before the first phone call.

gapSummary.gap: No special offers or seasonal promotions
gapSummary.impact: Orlando HVAC competitors frequently run promotions like '$49 AC Tune-Up' or '$500 Off New System Install.' Your site has no offers or deals. This gives homeowners no reason to pick you over a competitor offering a discount right now.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: Your 66 Google reviews are invisible to visitors, the main phone number doesn't work on mobile, and the site takes 8-12 seconds to load on a phone
impactEstimate.installNote: If even 1 of those was a system replacement, that's another $8,000-$15,000.

gapSummary.gap: Main phone number on homepage doesn't work on mobile
gapSummary.impact: The big phone number displayed on your homepage — 210-680-7037 — looks like it should be clickable, but tapping it on a phone does absolutely nothing. The button is broken. gapSummary.gap: Website takes 8-12 seconds to load on a phone

gapSummary.gap: No way to book online

gapSummary.gap: No tracking installed — flying completely blind

gapSummary.gap: Social media links go to Google+ — shut down in 2019
gapSummary.impact: Every page on your site has a link to Google+, which was permanently shut down over 6 years ago. Clicking it leads to a dead page. This tells visitors that nobody has touched this website in years, which undercuts the trust you've built with 66 five-star reviews.

gapSummary.gap: Website loads at two different addresses
gapSummary.impact: Both mydavco.com and www.mydavco.com show the same content with no redirect. Google sees these as two separate websites competing against each other. Your ranking power is split in half instead of building one strong listing.

gapSummary.gap: Security warning risk from mixed content
gapSummary.impact: Your site loads securely (HTTPS), but it tries to load an old review widget from an insecure source. Modern browsers block this, so the widget probably doesn't work. Worse, some browsers may show a 'Not Secure' warning to visitors — which scares off anyone about to call.

gapSummary.gap: No blog or educational content
gapSummary.impact: Your site has zero articles. gapSummary.impact: HVAC replacements cost $5,000-$15,000+. Many homeowners can't pay that upfront. Competitors who advertise '$89/month financing available' close more replacement jobs because they remove the cost barrier. You mention nothing about payment plans anywhere.

gapSummary.gap: Emergency messaging is buried and contradicts business hours

gapSummary.impact: Seven of your neighborhood pages have broken web addresses that end with trailing dashes. These pages are supposed to help you show up for searches in Hollywood Park, Cross Mountain, Grey Forest, and other areas you serve.

gapSummary.gap: A D3 globe animation loads on every page for no reason
gapSummary.impact: Every page loads a heavy animated globe visualization — a decorative element in the social media sidebar. This adds hundreds of kilobytes of unnecessary code to every single page. It provides zero value to an HVAC website and significantly slows down loading.

gapSummary.gap: Footer links to a dead website company

gapSummary.impact: San Antonio HVAC competitors frequently run promotions like '$49 AC Tune-Up' or '$50 Off Any Repair.' Your site has zero offers or deals. That gives homeowners no reason to call you right now instead of waiting or choosing a competitor running a sale.

gapSummary.gap: Your website tells Google it's written in Chinese
gapSummary.impact: Your website sends a signal to Google saying the content is in Chinese (zh-CN). It doesn't mean Google can't read the page, but it's a trust signal working against you in every local search.

gapSummary.gap: No description showing up in Google search results
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage has no description set for Google. This means Google makes up its own snippet when showing your site in search results — and it's usually random text pulled from the page. Competitors with clear descriptions that include their phone number and service area get more clicks because searchers know exactly what they're getting.

gapSummary.gap: Page title just says 'Home' instead of describing your business
gapSummary.impact: Your page title — the blue link people see in Google — says 'Home | tropicairheat' instead of describing what you do. The title is the single most important thing Google reads to understand what a page is about. 'Home' tells Google nothing about HVAC services in Arlington.

gapSummary.gap: Site takes over 9 seconds to load
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage took 9.3 seconds to load. The platform loads 62 separate code files on a single page. When an Arlington homeowner's AC breaks and they're searching on their phone, they're not waiting 9 seconds for a page to appear. They hit the back button and call whoever loads first.

gapSummary.impact: There's no way for customers to schedule an appointment online. Over 60% of homeowners prefer to book online, especially after business hours. gapSummary.gap: Dead Google+ social media link from 2019
gapSummary.impact: Your footer links to Google+, which was shut down by Google in April 2019 — over 6 years ago. Clicking it leads to a dead page. This makes your business look outdated and signals that nobody has updated this website in years.

gapSummary.gap: Browser tab shows Wix logo instead of your business
gapSummary.impact: The browser tab shows the generic Wix 'W' icon instead of your Tropic Air & Heat logo. This looks unprofessional and makes it harder for customers to find your tab when they have multiple sites open for comparison.

gapSummary.impact: Your entire website has just 6 pages. Competitors with 20-50 pages of content outrank you because Google sees them as more authoritative. Each of your services deserves its own dedicated page — AC repair, heating installation, maintenance, duct cleaning — not everything lumped together on one page.

gapSummary.gap: Analytics may not be working
gapSummary.impact: Your site references an old Google Analytics tracking code that Google stopped processing in July 2024. If this wasn't updated, you've had no working analytics for over a year — no idea how many people visit or what they do on your site.

gapSummary.impact: 9 out of 15 images on your homepage have no descriptions. Google can't understand what these images show, so they can't help your rankings. Adding descriptions helps you show up in Google Image search and improves accessibility for all visitors.

businessSummary.seasonalUrgency: Houston's brutal summer drives AC demand from April through October. Every day your website shows a San Francisco address is another day cautious homeowners are bouncing to competitors who look like real Houston businesses.

gapSummary.gap: Website says you're in San Francisco, CA — not Houston

gapSummary.gap: Wrong phone number showing — California area code instead of Houston
gapSummary.impact: The header shows a phone number with a (707) area code — that's Northern California, not Houston. gapSummary.gap: Wrong email address from template developer

gapSummary.impact: Three links in the 'Why Choose Us' section send visitors to a completely different HVAC company's demo website. Customers who click these links leave your site entirely and land on someone else's page. This is template content that was never updated with your own links.

gapSummary.gap: Code tells Google this site was built from a mass-produced template
gapSummary.impact: The hidden code Google reads shows the website author as a template company, with links to a construction company's demo site. This tells Google your site was mass-produced from a template rather than built for your business specifically — which can hurt trust and rankings.

gapSummary.gap: No tracking installed — completely blind
gapSummary.impact: There's no Google Analytics, no call tracking, no conversion tracking. You have zero data on how many people visit your site, which pages they look at, or where they come from. Without this data, there's no way to measure if anything you do for marketing is working.

gapSummary.gap: 49 Google reviews not displayed on the homepage
gapSummary.impact: Your site uses a review widget, but your 49 Google reviews at 4.8 stars are not shown on the homepage. The Google star rating is the single most powerful trust signal for HVAC customers — and it's hidden. Displaying your real rating and reviews would build instant trust with every visitor.

gapSummary.gap: Homepage is extremely heavy and slow
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage HTML file alone is 435 KB — about 4-5 times larger than it should be. On top of that, 45 code files are loading. When a Houston homeowner searches for emergency AC repair on a hot day, a slow-loading site means they move to the next search result.

gapSummary.gap: No way to book online

gapSummary.gap: No physical address shown anywhere
gapSummary.impact: Your site only shows 'West University Place, TX' — no street address. For a service business, showing a physical address builds trust and helps with local search rankings. Without it, your site looks like it could be anyone operating from anywhere.

gapSummary.gap: Internal links use insecure addresses
gapSummary.impact: Many links throughout your site use 'http://' instead of 'https://' — including links to your services, homepage, and contact pages. Each of these creates an unnecessary redirect that slows loading and looks sloppy to Google.

gapSummary.impact: The structured data Google reads is missing your phone number, full address, and service area. It also incorrectly lists hours as 9-5 when your description says 24/7. This conflicting information confuses Google about when you're available and can show wrong hours in search results.

gapSummary.gap: No emergency or 24/7 messaging visible on the page
gapSummary.impact: Your description in Google mentions '24/7 availability' but this is nowhere visible on the actual homepage. If you offer emergency service, it should be the first thing a stressed homeowner sees. If you don't, the Google description is misleading customers.

gapSummary.gap: Shared links show no preview image
gapSummary.impact: When someone shares your website on Facebook, Twitter, or in a text message, no preview image appears. Shared links look like plain text with no visual — which gets far fewer clicks than a link with a branded image.

gapSummary.gap: Blog posts exist but don't target Houston searches
gapSummary.impact: You have 14 blog posts — which is a good start. But the topics are extremely generic ('What is an HVAC system?') and don't target Houston-area searches. None mention Houston, West University Place, or any local neighborhoods. Rewriting these to target real customer searches in Houston would help them actually rank.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: Your 'Get Directions' link sends customers to Virginia, your phone number is hidden on phones, and a 40MB video makes the site take 8+ seconds to load
impactEstimate.installNote: If even 1 of those was a system replacement, that's another $8,000-$15,000.

businessSummary.oneLiner: You have a strong 4.7-star reputation with 49 Google reviews in Jacksonville. But your website may be costing you HVAC calls. We found several issues that could be sending potential customers to competitors instead.
topThreeGaps: Your 'Get Directions' link sends customers to a completely different business in Virginia — 700 miles away from Jacksonville
Your phone number is hidden on phones and tablets — the devices most people use to call an HVAC company
A 40MB video auto-loads on every visit, making your site take 8+ seconds to appear on a phone
businessSummary.competitivePosition: You have a solid reputation — 49 reviews at 4.7 stars in Jacksonville shows customers trust your work. But your website has critical issues that are actively preventing phone calls. The wrong directions link and hidden phone number are the most urgent because they directly block customers from reaching you.
businessSummary.seasonalUrgency: AC demand in Jacksonville runs nearly year-round but peaks from May through September. Every week these issues stay open — especially the hidden phone number and wrong directions — is another week of lost emergency calls and system replacements.
businessSummary.recommendedFirstProject: Fix the Google Maps link so it points to your Jacksonville business instead of Virginia, make your phone number visible on phones and tablets, and replace the 40MB video with a lightweight image so your site loads in seconds instead of minutes.
businessSummary.growthOpportunity: Full website optimization with pages targeting Jacksonville neighborhoods (Mandarin, Southside, Riverside, Orange Park), Google Reviews widget showing your 4.7-star rating, online booking, a blog targeting local HVAC questions, and a financing page to close more system replacement jobs.

gapSummary.gap: Get Directions sends customers to a business in Virginia — 700 miles away
gapSummary.impact: The 'Get Directions' link in your footer points to 'Indoor Air Quality Medics' in Woodbridge, Virginia — a completely different company 700 miles from Jacksonville. This appears on every single page. Anyone who clicks it gets directions to Virginia instead of your shop. This is a devastating trust issue that needs to be fixed today.

gapSummary.gap: Phone number is hidden on phones and tablets
gapSummary.impact: Your phone number has a setting that makes it completely invisible on phones and tablets. The only visible phone number on mobile is buried in the footer. When someone's AC breaks and they find your site on their phone, the phone number should be the most obvious thing they see — not hidden.

gapSummary.gap: A 40MB video auto-loads on every visit
gapSummary.impact: A 40MB video file loads as a background on your homepage — that's larger than most entire websites. On a phone with spotty signal, this could take 30+ seconds to download and eats through mobile data plans. Most visitors won't even notice it's a video versus a still image, but they will notice the page takes forever to load.

gapSummary.gap: Homepage takes 8+ seconds to load
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage took over 8 seconds just to start loading. It loads 47 style files, 21 code files, and that 40MB video. For a Jacksonville homeowner whose AC just broke at 2 PM in August, waiting 8 seconds means hitting the back button and calling the next company on Google.

gapSummary.gap: Google can't properly identify your business type
gapSummary.impact: Google uses special code to understand that you're an HVAC business with a physical address, phone number, and service area. Your site only has generic code that doesn't identify you as a local HVAC company. Competitors with proper business details in their code show star ratings, hours, and click-to-call buttons in Google results. You show up as a plain blue link.

gapSummary.gap: 49 Google reviews not displayed on your website
gapSummary.impact: You have 49 Google reviews at 4.7 stars — that's excellent trust proof. But those reviews aren't displayed directly on your site. The reviews section depends on a third-party widget that loads from an outside server. If that service goes down, visitors see nothing. Showing your real Google rating directly on the homepage would instantly build trust.

gapSummary.gap: Contact form depends entirely on a third-party service
gapSummary.impact: Your contact form loads from an outside service — if that company has a slow day, changes their pricing, or goes down, your contact form disappears completely. Your main way to capture leads should not depend on a service you don't control.

gapSummary.gap: No emergency or 24/7 messaging
gapSummary.impact: HVAC emergencies happen at all hours in Florida. When someone's AC dies at 2 AM in summer, they search for 'emergency HVAC Jacksonville.' Your website has zero mention of emergency service, after-hours availability, or same-day service. Even saying 'same-day appointments available' would give people a reason to choose you.

gapSummary.gap: No pages targeting Jacksonville neighborhoods
gapSummary.impact: Jacksonville is the largest city by area in the lower 48 states. People search for 'AC repair Mandarin,' 'HVAC Southside Jacksonville,' and 'heating repair Orange Park.' You have zero pages targeting any neighborhood or surrounding city. Each neighborhood page is a chance to rank for local searches competitors are already targeting.

gapSummary.gap: No blog or educational content
gapSummary.impact: There's no blog on your site. Articles like 'how often should I change my air filter' or 'signs your AC needs repair' attract people searching for HVAC questions. These visitors may not need service today, but when they do, you're already the company they trust. Competitors who blog regularly outrank you over time.

gapSummary.gap: No financing page or payment options
gapSummary.impact: An AC replacement in Jacksonville costs $4,000-$12,000. Many homeowners can't pay that upfront. Competitors who advertise financing close more replacement jobs because they remove the cost barrier. You mention nothing about financing or payment plans anywhere.

gapSummary.gap: No tracking installed — flying blind
gapSummary.impact: There's no Google Analytics or any tracking on your site. You have no idea how many people visit, which pages they look at, or if your marketing is working. You're making business decisions about your online presence with zero data.

gapSummary.gap: One-third of images have no descriptions for Google
gapSummary.impact: 6 out of 17 images on your homepage have no descriptions. Google can't understand what these images show, so they can't help your rankings. Adding descriptions helps you show up in Google Image search for searches like 'HVAC company Jacksonville.'

gapSummary.gap: Two main headings on one page confuses Google
gapSummary.impact: Your homepage has two main headings — Google looks at the main heading to understand what a page is about. Having two sends mixed signals. The second one ('Put us to work') says nothing about HVAC or Jacksonville.

gapSummary.gap: Google description is a generic service list
gapSummary.impact: Your description in Google search results is just a plain list of services with no personality, no phone number, and no reason to click. A good description should make someone pick you over the other nine results on the page.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: No way to call from the website, no booking option, no contact form, and your 11,798 Google reviews are invisible to visitors
impactEstimate.installNote: Even 1-2 replacement jobs from those missed leads could mean another $16,000-$30,000.

businessSummary.oneLiner: You have a strong 4.8-star reputation with 11,798 Google reviews in Mesa. But your website may be costing you HVAC calls. We found several issues that could be sending potential customers to competitors instead.
topThreeGaps: Your 11,798 Google reviews are nowhere on your website — visitors never see the reputation you've built
Your phone number isn't clickable and doesn't match your Google Business Profile — customers can't tap to call and Google may be confused about your business
There's no way to book, no contact form, and no clear call-to-action — visitors have to figure out how to reach you on their own
businessSummary.competitivePosition: With 11,798 reviews at 4.8 stars, you have one of the strongest reputations in the Mesa HVAC market. But your website isn't converting that reputation into calls. Competitors with cleaner sites and visible booking options are capturing customers who should be calling you.
businessSummary.seasonalUrgency: AC demand in Mesa spikes by March — earlier than most markets because of the desert heat. Every week these gaps stay open is another week competitors capture the emergency calls and replacement jobs that should be yours.
businessSummary.recommendedFirstProject: Make your phone number clickable and match it to your Google Business Profile so customers can tap to call and Google sees one consistent business. Add a clear call-to-action above the fold so visitors know exactly how to reach you.
businessSummary.growthOpportunity: Full website overhaul with a review widget showing your 11,798 Google reviews, online scheduling for after-hours bookings, service area pages targeting Mesa, Gilbert, Chandler, Tempe, and surrounding cities, and a contact form so visitors have multiple ways to reach you.

gapSummary.gap: 11,798 Google reviews not showing on your website
gapSummary.impact: You've earned a 4.8-star rating with 11,798 reviews — that's exceptional in the Mesa market. But none of them appear on your website. When a homeowner is comparing HVAC companies, they pick the one showing real customer feedback. Right now, visitors have to leave your site and search Google separately to see what your customers think.

gapSummary.gap: Phone number on website doesn't match Google
gapSummary.impact: The phone number on your website is different from the one on your Google Business Profile. Google uses this to verify and rank local businesses — when the numbers don't match, Google has less confidence in your listing. This can push you lower in local search results right when Mesa homeowners are searching for HVAC help.

gapSummary.gap: Phone number isn't clickable on mobile
gapSummary.impact: When someone visits your site on their phone — which is most people — they can't tap your phone number to call. They'd have to memorize it, switch apps, and dial manually. Most won't bother. They'll call whichever competitor makes it easy.

gapSummary.gap: No clear way to call or book above the fold
gapSummary.impact: When someone lands on your homepage, there's no obvious next step — no 'Call Now', 'Book Online', or 'Get a Quote' button visible without scrolling. In Mesa's heat, a homeowner with a broken AC isn't going to scroll around. They'll hit the back button and call whoever makes it easiest.

gapSummary.gap: No way to book online
gapSummary.impact: There's no way to schedule an appointment from your website. Most people prefer to book when it's convenient — including late at night or on weekends. After-hours visitors go to whichever competitor lets them book at 11 PM.

gapSummary.gap: No contact form on the website
gapSummary.impact: Some customers prefer writing over calling — especially for non-urgent requests like maintenance plans or quotes. Without a contact form, you're missing these leads entirely. They go to competitors who give them options.

gapSummary.gap: No after-hours lead capture
gapSummary.impact: When someone visits your site after business hours, there's no way to capture their information. No form, no booking widget, no chat. They move on to a competitor who can take their request at 10 PM.

gapSummary.gap: Your description in Google search results is missing
gapSummary.impact: Your site doesn't have a description set for Google search results. Google will pick random text from your page instead — which may not be compelling. A clear description like 'Mesa's top-rated HVAC company with 11,798 five-star reviews' could dramatically increase clicks from search.

gapSummary.gap: No title tag set for search results
gapSummary.impact: Your website doesn't have a proper title for Google search results. This is one of the most basic things Google looks at to understand what your page is about. Without it, you're invisible for searches like 'HVAC Mesa AZ' or 'AC repair Mesa'.

gapSummary.gap: No pages targeting cities you serve
gapSummary.impact: There are zero pages about the cities you serve. When someone searches 'AC repair Gilbert' or 'HVAC Chandler', your site has nothing to show. Competitors with location-specific pages show up — you don't.

gapSummary.gap: No license number displayed
gapSummary.impact: Arizona requires HVAC contractors to be licensed, and homeowners want to verify it. When your license number isn't on your website, it raises questions — even if you're fully licensed. Competitors who display theirs look more trustworthy.

gapSummary.gap: Google can't read your business details from the code
gapSummary.impact: Your website doesn't include structured business information that Google can read directly. This means Google has to guess your business name, address, phone, and hours from the page content — and it doesn't always guess right.

gapSummary.gap: Website loads at two different addresses
gapSummary.impact: Your website works at both semperfiheatingcooling.com and www.semperfiheatingcooling.com with no redirect. Google may see these as two separate websites competing against each other, splitting your ranking power in half.

gapSummary.gap: No trust badges or certifications shown
gapSummary.impact: No BBB badge, manufacturer certifications, or industry affiliations are visible on your website. These visual trust signals help homeowners feel confident choosing you over a competitor — especially when they're spending thousands on a system.

gapSummary.gap: No 'Licensed & Insured' messaging
gapSummary.impact: Your website doesn't mention that you're licensed and insured. This is one of the first things homeowners look for — especially for expensive jobs like system replacements. Adding it takes minutes and immediately builds trust.

gapSummary.gap: No blog or educational content
gapSummary.impact: Your site has no blog or helpful articles. Content about common HVAC problems, maintenance tips, and seasonal guides helps Google rank you for the questions Mesa homeowners are actually searching.

gapSummary.gap: None of your images have descriptions for Google
gapSummary.impact: Zero percent of your images have descriptions (alt text). Google uses these to understand what's in your images and can show them in image search results. Without them, you're missing another way for customers to find you.

gapSummary.gap: No video content on site
gapSummary.impact: Sites with video tend to keep visitors longer and build more trust. A simple walkthrough of your team, a job in progress, or a customer testimonial can make a real difference in converting visitors to callers.

gapSummary.gap: No sticky phone button on mobile
gapSummary.impact: When visitors scroll on their phone, the phone number disappears. A sticky 'Call Now' button that stays visible while scrolling makes it effortless to reach you at any point.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: Phone number mismatch with Google, no contact form, and no license number showing — all eroding trust before visitors even call
impactEstimate.installNote: Even 1-2 replacement jobs from those missed leads could mean another $12,000-$25,000.

gapSummary.impact: If you're running ads or showing up in search results, visitors arrive at your site expecting to see proof that you're trustworthy — reviews, license numbers, certifications. Without these visible, you're paying to get people to your door but not giving them a reason to knock.

gapSummary.gap: No license number displayed on your website
gapSummary.impact: Florida homeowners expect to see a contractor's license number before hiring. It's a basic trust signal — especially for big-ticket jobs like system replacements. Without it visible on your site, some visitors may question whether you're properly licensed.

gapSummary.gap: No industry trust badges or certifications shown
gapSummary.impact: Badges like NATE Certified, BBB Accredited, or manufacturer authorizations give visitors instant confidence. Your competitors who display these badges look more credible at first glance — even if your team is more qualified.

gapSummary.gap: No specials or coupons page
gapSummary.impact: Many homeowners search specifically for HVAC deals or coupons before choosing a company. A dedicated specials page gives you another way to show up in search results and gives price-conscious customers a reason to pick you over the competition.

gapSummary.gap: No video content on website
gapSummary.impact: Video builds trust faster than text. A quick intro from the owner, a technician walk-through, or a customer testimonial video helps visitors feel like they know your team before they call. Competitors using video on their sites convert more visitors into calls.

gapSummary.gap: Running ads without call tracking
gapSummary.impact: If you're spending money on Google Ads or other advertising, you need to know which ads are driving phone calls. Without call tracking, you're flying blind — spending money but unable to tell what's working and what's wasted.

gapSummary.gap: Most images are missing descriptions for Google
gapSummary.impact: Only 43% of your images have descriptions (alt text). Google uses these to understand and rank your images in search results. Better coverage means more ways for Tampa Bay homeowners to find you when searching for HVAC services.

gapSummary.gap: 6 images could be smaller for faster loading
gapSummary.impact: Several images on your site are larger than they need to be, which adds to the 8.8-second load time. Compressing and resizing these images is one of the easiest ways to make your site load faster — and keep more visitors from bouncing.
